title: VMware NSX Edge Transport Node Performance: North-South Gateway Throughput, BGP/IPSec/VPN Session Health, and Load-Balancer Pool Capacity
exclusions: Host transport node connectivity / GENEVE TEP tunnel monitoring (use UC-18.2.5 — different transport-node category); Distributed firewall east-west traffic (use UC-18.2.1 — runs in ESXi vsip kernel module, not Edge); VMware Avi Load Balancer (formerly Avi Vantage) — separate product from native NSX LB; VMware NSX SD-WAN (Velocloud) — different product line; third-party load balancers (F5 Big-IP, Citrix ADC) hosted alongside NSX — separate UCs in cat-18.3 / cat-15; physical-fabric Cisco Nexus / Arista / Juniper BGP peering on the upstream-router side (those events flow into UC-18.4.* or cat-15 use cases).
dataSources: index=vmware / index=vmware_nsx / index=network_ops (rename per governance) combining Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt (Splunkbase 4856) vmware:nsxt:syslog tails carrying Edge /var/log/syslog (BGP[NEIGHBOR]:, lb[VIRTUAL_SERVER]:, ipsec[]:, charon) plus /var/log/edgexb/*.log structured exporters; custom HTTPS/HEC feeds indexed as vmware:nsxt:edge_status, vmware:nsxt:edge_bgp, vmware:nsxt:edge_lb, vmware:nsxt:edge_dataplane, vmware:nsxt:edge_vpn mirroring Policy endpoints (tier-0s/{t0-id}, locale-services/{ls-id}/bgp/neighbors/{n-id}/status, lb-services/{lbs-id}/lb-pools/{pool-id}/members, lb-services/{lbs-id}/statistics, transport-nodes/{tn-id}/status, edge-clusters/{cluster-id}/sessions); optional Splunk Connect for Syslog receivers plus Splunk_TA_vmware (Splunkbase 1810) vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter joins when correlating Edge VM sizing versus dataplane saturation.
app: Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) deployed where vmware:nsxt:syslog parsing executes; Splunk_TA_vmware ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) optional for vcenter joins; scripted HTTPS collectors authenticating POST /api/session/create cookies or mutual TLS identities covering Policy infra/tier-0s/lb-services/edge-clusters reads enumerated inside VMware NSX API references.
spl:
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_bgp" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_lb" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_dataplane" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_vpn" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_status" OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(bgp\\[neighbor\\]|lb\\[virtual_server\\]|ipsec|charon|ikev2)")))
earliest=-4h@m
| eval ls_id=trim(coalesce(locale_services_id, ls_uuid, ""))
| eval nbr_id=trim(coalesce(neighbor_id, bgp_neighbor_id, ""))
| eval bgp_neighbor_state=upper(trim(coalesce(connection_state, bgp_session_state, neighbor_state, "")))
| eval pool_member_status=upper(trim(coalesce(member_admin_state, backend_health, pool_member_state, "")))
| eval lb_virtual_server_id=trim(coalesce(vs_policy_path, virtual_server_display_name, vs_name, ""))
| eval lbs_id=trim(coalesce(load_balancer_service_path, lbs_parent_id, ""))
| eval tier_0_gateway_id=trim(coalesce(tier0_policy_path, tier0_id, ""))
| eval tier_1_gateway_id=trim(coalesce(tier1_policy_path, tier1_id, ""))
| eval edge_transport_node_id=trim(coalesce(edge_node_name, entity_id, hostname, ""))
| eval dataplane_pps=tonumber(coalesce(fastpath_pps, rx_tx_pps_aggregate, total_pps, "0"))
| eval dataplane_gbps=tonumber(coalesce(aggregate_gbps, fabric_uplink_gbps, "0"))
| eval dataplane_drops=tonumber(coalesce(total_drop_packets, dpdk_drop_count, "0"))
| eval ipsec_tunnel_count=tonumber(coalesce(ike_phase2_sa_count, active_ipsec_sessions, "0"))
| eval edge_cpu_pct=tonumber(coalesce(dataplane_cpu_util, edge_cpu_pct, combined_cpu, "0"))
| eval runtime_status=upper(trim(coalesce(edge_runtime, cluster_status_line, "")))
| eval edge_risk=case(
    bgp_neighbor_state IN ("IDLE","CONNECT","ACTIVE","OPENSENT","OPENCONFIRM") OR (match(sourcetype,"edge_bgp") AND bgp_neighbor_state!="ESTABLISHED" AND isnotnull(bgp_neighbor_state)), "tier0_bgp_peer_not_established",
    pool_member_status IN ("DOWN","DISABLED","UNUSED","GRACEFUL_DISABLED"), "native_lb_pool_member_bad",
    isnotnull(dataplane_drops) AND dataplane_drops>10000 AND dataplane_pps>50000 AND (dataplane_drops/dataplane_pps)>0.01, "dpdk_drop_ratio_high",
    runtime_status IN ("DOWN","DEGRADED","UNKNOWN"), "edge_appliance_runtime_bad",
    edge_cpu_pct>92, "edge_compute_hot",
    match(_raw,"(?i)(preempt|split.?brain|standby.*active|bfd)"), "edge_ha_cluster_event",
    ipsec_tunnel_count==0 AND match(sourcetype,"edge_vpn"), "ipsec_inventory_empty_anomaly",
    null())
| where isnotnull(edge_risk)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats latest(_time) as last_event,
    latest(bgp_neighbor_state) as bgp_state_latest,
    latest(pool_member_status) as lb_pool_latest,
    latest(lb_virtual_server_id) as vs_latest,
    latest(tier_0_gateway_id) as tier0_latest,
    latest(tier_1_gateway_id) as tier1_latest,
    max(dataplane_gbps) as gbps_peak,
    max(dataplane_pps) as pps_peak,
    max(dataplane_drops) as drop_peak,
    max(edge_cpu_pct) as cpu_peak,
    latest(ipsec_tunnel_count) as vpn_sessions_latest,
    latest(runtime_status) as runtime_latest,
    values(edge_transport_node_id) as edge_nodes,
    values(nbr_id) as bgp_neighbor_ids,
    values(lbs_id) as lbs_ids,
    values(ls_id) as locale_services_seen
  by _time edge_risk
| sort -last_event
| table _time edge_risk edge_nodes tier0_latest tier1_latest bgp_state_latest lb_pool_latest vs_latest gbps_peak pps_peak drop_peak cpu_peak vpn_sessions_latest runtime_latest bgp_neighbor_ids locale_services_seen lbs_ids last_event
description: Aggregates VMware NSX Edge transport-node telemetry covering Tier-0 Gateway north-south routing toward DC core/WAN, Tier-1 Gateway downstream segments, Edge cluster CPU/datapath saturation, DPDK fastpath pps/gbps/drops counters, BGP neighbor finite-state telemetry (Established, Active, Idle, Connect, OpenSent, OpenConfirm versus RFC 4271 expectations), IPSec/IKEv2 tunnel inventories (phase-1 / phase-2 SA counts via edge-clusters/{cluster-id}/sessions snapshots), native Layer-4/Layer-7 load balancer lb-services/lb-pools/lb-virtual-servers health (UP/DOWN/DISABLED/GRACEFUL_DISABLED/UNUSED), plus active-standby Edge HA failover fingerprints (preemption, split-brain wording, BFD-driven takeover cues)—distinct from east-west vsip Distributed Firewall hits or GENEVE TEP tunnel posture monitored elsewhere.
value: Tier-0 SR outages strand north-south flows before Tier-1 tenants notice dashboards wobble—BGP Established jitter, native balancer DOWN storms, IKE tunnel churn, DPDK drop spikes, and Edge cluster runtime regressions rarely advertise heroically inside Tier-1 application charts alone. Folding locale_services-scoped BGP pulls, lb-services/statistics mirrors, vmware:nsxt:edge_dataplane fastpath ratios, vmware:nsxt:edge_vpn inventories, syslog BGP/LB/IPSec anecdotes, and transport-node status snapshots into Splunk lets NetOps prioritize Tier-0 north-south investigations—routing ECMP active-active layouts, VPN IKE lifetime tweaks, LB monitor edits—instead of rerouting blame toward Distributed Firewall siblings covering east-west microsegmentation scopes handled under UC-18.2.1 narratives.
implementation: Deploy Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt syslog tails plus scripted HTTPS/HEC collectors emitting vmware:nsxt:edge_status, vmware:nsxt:edge_bgp, vmware:nsxt:edge_lb, vmware:nsxt:edge_dataplane, vmware:nsxt:edge_vpn JSON keyed by tier_0_gateway_id, tier_1_gateway_id, locale_services identifiers, pool_member_status, bgp_neighbor_state, dataplane counters, ipsec_tunnel_count, optional Splunk Connect for Syslog scaling plus Splunk_TA_vmware joins—schedule macros/nsxt_edge_ns_risk-wrapped alerts tied to edge_risk buckets excluding Distributed Firewall/GENEVE sibling scopes intentionally.
visualization: (1) Heatmap: edge_risk versus tier0_latest coloured by drop_peak. (2) Timechart: gbps_peak/pps_peak stacked with vpn_sessions_latest overlays annotated when edge_ha_cluster_event spikes. (3) Table: edge_nodes, tier0_latest, tier1_latest, bgp_state_latest, lb_pool_latest, vs_latest, runtime_latest, gbps_peak, pps_peak, drop_peak. (4) Single-value: percentage of edge_risk rows absent tier0_bgp_peer_not_established/native_lb_pool_member_bad/dpdk_drop_ratio_high classes trailing six hours post-suppression. (5) Geomap optional only when site_code enrichment exists—otherwise facet by metro/campus tokens sourced via CMDB lookups tied to Edge clusters.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance once FIELDALIAS maps dataplane_gbps, dataplane_pps, edge_cpu_pct, runtime_status into vendor-neutral cpu_load_percent/service_health semantics compatible with Infrastructure dashboards; accelerate Network_Traffic when bgp_neighbor_state, pool_member_status, ipsec_tunnel_count tuples normalize session facets referencing tier_0_gateway_id anchors.
knownFalsePositives: **BGP graceful-restart rehearsal noise** — Datacenter-core upgrades intentionally stagger BGP helper timers so Established peers briefly flirt with Active / Idle states—Splunk tier0_bgp_peer_not_established rows spike though WAN optics remain deliberate—suppress windows anchored to CRQ stamps referencing graceful-restart / MAINT_MODE bridges before accusing Tier-0 misconfiguration alone. **Edge maintenance churn masking dataplane dips** — Entering Maintenance Mode on Edge appliances triggers active-standby takeover shifting Tier-0 SR ownership—throughput gauges tumble although north-south paths recover via standby peers—tie edge_compute_hot / gbps_peak drops to Edge evacuation bridge calendars rather than blackhole hypotheses instantly. **LB probe subnet mismatches mid-refactor** — ACTIVE HTTP / HTTPS monitors aimed at stale management IPs during lb-pools subnet migrations briefly stamp pool_member_status DOWN across every backend though applications answer correctly—coordinate monitor / pool edits recorded inside CMDB tickets before paging Tier-1 application squads. **IKEv2 DPD timeouts during WAN MTU realignment** — Ipsec dpd-timeout retries coincide with PMTUD / MSS harmonisation exercises across WAN MTU bridges—tunnel-down syslog bursts mimic outages although IKE phase-2 SA counts stabilize seconds later once strongSwan / charon timers converge—suppress IPSEC_TUNNEL_DOWN overlays aligned with WAN bridge approvals referencing Tier-0 VPN attachments. **Bare-metal NIC firmware flashes resembling cluster outages** — Rolling NIC firmware / driver refreshes on bare-metal Edge nodes briefly reset dataplane counters—Splunk runtime_latest / drop_peak anomalies mimic catastrophic Edge failures despite Tier-1 tenants riding unaffected peers inside Edge clusters hosting ECMP active-active layouts—cross-check hardware bridge notices before split-brain escalations hit Tier-0 routing SMEs nightly. **ECMP convergence lag after Tier-0 attribute tweaks** — Editing BGP path-attribute / multi-hop knobs toward upstream Nexus / Arista routers introduces route installation latency across ECMP active-active Tier-0 locale_services attachments—temporary blackhole / asymmetric forwarding narratives surface inside dataplane_drops / pps_peak jitter although Established BGP peers never flap—extend smoothing filters referencing routing bridge approvals capturing BGP policy / route-map edits tied to Tier-0 north-south fabrics alone.
hardware: VMware NSX Edge transport nodes are deployed as either VM-form-factor (running on ESXi 7.0/8.0/9.0 hypervisor with VM appliance sizing Small/Medium/Large/Extra-Large — Large = 8 vCPU / 32 GB RAM with 4 NICs typical for production) or bare-metal Edge (Dell PowerEdge R650/R750/R760 with 2-4× 25/100 GbE NICs, supporting up to 80 Gbps throughput per node). Edge clusters typically deploy 2 nodes for active-standby HA or up to 10 nodes for active-active ECMP routing. NSX Manager 3-node cluster (8 vCPU / 32 GB RAM / 200 GB disk per node) hosts the policy and management plane that pushes Edge configuration. Physical-fabric BGP peering typically uses Cisco Nexus / Arista / Juniper top-of-rack as next-hop physical routers.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) wherever **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** parsing runs for **VMware NSX 4.2**, **VMware NSX 4.1**, or **NSX-T 3.2.x**, preserving `BGP[NEIGHBOR]:`, `lb[VIRTUAL_SERVER]:`, `ipsec[]:`, `charon`, `edgexb` exporters tied to Edge syslog conventions.
• **Optional vSphere joins** — Deploy **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) when **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** sizing (**Small/Medium/Large/Extra-Large**) must correlate `edge_cpu_pct` spikes versus undersized appliance profiles.
• **HTTPS collectors + RBAC** — Authenticate `POST /api/session/create` cookies or mutual TLS identities covering `infra/tier-0s` locale-services BGP neighbors (`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/tier-0s/{t0-id}/locale-services/{ls-id}/bgp/neighbors/{n-id}/status`) plus `lb-services` / `lb-pools` pulls enumerated inside VMware `vpn.html` / `load-balancer.html` chapters.
• **Custom sourcetypes** — Reserve **`vmware:nsxt:edge_status`**, ****vmware:nsxt:edge_bgp****, **`vmware:nsxt:edge_lb`**, **`vmware:nsxt:edge_dataplane`**, ****vmware:nsxt:edge_vpn**** keyed by `tier_0_gateway_id`, `tier_1_gateway_id`, `pool_member_status`, `bgp_neighbor_state`, dataplane `pps`/`gbps`/`drops`, `ipsec_tunnel_count`; optionally front syslog via `Splunk Connect for Syslog` when `RFC 3164`/`5424` relays preserve `PRI` tokens end-to-end.
• **Parsing hygiene** — Normalize camelCase REST versus syslog tokens inside `FIELDALIAS` overlays bundled into your `custom Splunk app` instead of rewriting `Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt` `default/` without change control.
• **Poll pacing** — Poll **45–180** second intervals balancing Established BGP visibility with **Manager API** 429/503 backoff etiquette per Broadcom operational guidance.

**Step 1 — Wire Edge ingestion**
(1) **Syslog relays** — Tail `/var/log/syslog` + `/var/log/edgexb/*.log`; reject relays stripping `PRI` headers needed for `vmware:nsxt:syslog` routing.
(2) **Alarm → HEC** — Map `EDGE_NODE_DOWN`, `BGP_NEIGHBOR_DOWN`, `LB_POOL_MEMBER_DOWN`, `IPSEC_TUNNEL_DOWN` alarms into `HEC` payloads carrying `edge_cluster_id`, `tier0_path` metadata matching Policy inventory paths.
(3) **Policy pulls** — Schedule HTTPS jobs over `GET .../edge-clusters`, `.../edge-transport-nodes`, `tier-0s/{t0-id}`, `locale-services/{ls-id}/bgp/neighbors`, `lb-services/{lbs-id}/lb-virtual-servers`, `lb-pools/{pool-id}/members`, aligning **Tier-0 Gateway** `locale_services` scopes with **Tier-1** downstream routers documented inside `tier-0-gateways.html` references.
(4) **VPN inventories** — Mirror `GET /api/v1/edge-clusters/{cluster-id}/sessions` feeds capturing `IKEv2` phase-1 / phase-2 counts referenced alongside Policy IPSec attachments (`vpn.html`).
(5) **Dataplane exporters** — Cron `get dataplane stats` snapshots into `vmware:nsxt:edge_dataplane` with jitter so `DPDK` counters remain coherent across active-active **ECMP** **Tier-0** peers.

**Step 2 — Promote SPL into macros**
Paste the **`spl`** payload once **`index=`** / **`sourcetype=`** contracts align—factor **`eval edge_risk`** into **`macros/nsxt_edge_ns_risk`** so threshold edits propagate uniformly.

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_bgp" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_lb" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_dataplane" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_vpn" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_status" OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(bgp\\[neighbor\\]|lb\\[virtual_server\\]|ipsec|charon|ikev2)")))
earliest=-4h@m
| eval ls_id=trim(coalesce(locale_services_id, ls_uuid, ""))
| eval nbr_id=trim(coalesce(neighbor_id, bgp_neighbor_id, ""))
| eval bgp_neighbor_state=upper(trim(coalesce(connection_state, bgp_session_state, neighbor_state, "")))
| eval pool_member_status=upper(trim(coalesce(member_admin_state, backend_health, pool_member_state, "")))
| eval lb_virtual_server_id=trim(coalesce(vs_policy_path, virtual_server_display_name, vs_name, ""))
| eval lbs_id=trim(coalesce(load_balancer_service_path, lbs_parent_id, ""))
| eval tier_0_gateway_id=trim(coalesce(tier0_policy_path, tier0_id, ""))
| eval tier_1_gateway_id=trim(coalesce(tier1_policy_path, tier1_id, ""))
| eval edge_transport_node_id=trim(coalesce(edge_node_name, entity_id, hostname, ""))
| eval dataplane_pps=tonumber(coalesce(fastpath_pps, rx_tx_pps_aggregate, total_pps, "0"))
| eval dataplane_gbps=tonumber(coalesce(aggregate_gbps, fabric_uplink_gbps, "0"))
| eval dataplane_drops=tonumber(coalesce(total_drop_packets, dpdk_drop_count, "0"))
| eval ipsec_tunnel_count=tonumber(coalesce(ike_phase2_sa_count, active_ipsec_sessions, "0"))
| eval edge_cpu_pct=tonumber(coalesce(dataplane_cpu_util, edge_cpu_pct, combined_cpu, "0"))
| eval runtime_status=upper(trim(coalesce(edge_runtime, cluster_status_line, "")))
| eval edge_risk=case(
    bgp_neighbor_state IN ("IDLE","CONNECT","ACTIVE","OPENSENT","OPENCONFIRM") OR (match(sourcetype,"edge_bgp") AND bgp_neighbor_state!="ESTABLISHED" AND isnotnull(bgp_neighbor_state)), "tier0_bgp_peer_not_established",
    pool_member_status IN ("DOWN","DISABLED","UNUSED","GRACEFUL_DISABLED"), "native_lb_pool_member_bad",
    isnotnull(dataplane_drops) AND dataplane_drops>10000 AND dataplane_pps>50000 AND (dataplane_drops/dataplane_pps)>0.01, "dpdk_drop_ratio_high",
    runtime_status IN ("DOWN","DEGRADED","UNKNOWN"), "edge_appliance_runtime_bad",
    edge_cpu_pct>92, "edge_compute_hot",
    match(_raw,"(?i)(preempt|split.?brain|standby.*active|bfd)"), "edge_ha_cluster_event",
    ipsec_tunnel_count==0 AND match(sourcetype,"edge_vpn"), "ipsec_inventory_empty_anomaly",
    null())
| where isnotnull(edge_risk)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats latest(_time) as last_event,
    latest(bgp_neighbor_state) as bgp_state_latest,
    latest(pool_member_status) as lb_pool_latest,
    latest(lb_virtual_server_id) as vs_latest,
    latest(tier_0_gateway_id) as tier0_latest,
    latest(tier_1_gateway_id) as tier1_latest,
    max(dataplane_gbps) as gbps_peak,
    max(dataplane_pps) as pps_peak,
    max(dataplane_drops) as drop_peak,
    max(edge_cpu_pct) as cpu_peak,
    latest(ipsec_tunnel_count) as vpn_sessions_latest,
    latest(runtime_status) as runtime_latest,
    values(edge_transport_node_id) as edge_nodes,
    values(nbr_id) as bgp_neighbor_ids,
    values(lbs_id) as lbs_ids,
    values(ls_id) as locale_services_seen
  by _time edge_risk
| sort -last_event
| table _time edge_risk edge_nodes tier0_latest tier1_latest bgp_state_latest lb_pool_latest vs_latest gbps_peak pps_peak drop_peak cpu_peak vpn_sessions_latest runtime_latest bgp_neighbor_ids locale_services_seen lbs_ids last_event
```

**Search logic notes**
• **`tier0_bgp_peer_not_established`** — BGP finite-state machines linger outside Established despite optics—validate `locale_services_seen` / `bgp_neighbor_ids` mappings before blaming DC-core alone.
• **`native_lb_pool_member_bad`** — Flags DOWN, DISABLED, UNUSED, GRACEFUL_DISABLED `pool_member_status` values sourced from `lb-pools` Policy APIs—not **`vsip`** **Distributed Firewall** telemetry.
• **`dpdk_drop_ratio_high`** — Uses `dataplane_drops`/`dataplane_pps` ratio; pair with `edge_compute_hot` to catch undersized Medium appliances versus north-south spikes.
• **`edge_ha_cluster_event`** — Pulls syslog tokens (**preempt**, split-brain, `BFD` takeover) for active-standby Edge HA—orthogonal to GENEVE/TEP UC-18.2.5 scopes.
• **`ipsec_inventory_empty_anomaly`** — Surfaces truncated `edge_vpn` feeds (HTTP 429/502) masquerading as zero tunnel rows.

Macro hygiene — Version-control **`macros/nsxt_edge_ns_risk`** beside savedsearches for audit replay of **`eval`** semantics.

**Step 3 — Validate in VMware consoles**
(1) Compare `bgp_state_latest` with **Networking → Tier-0 Gateways → BGP** neighbor states (Established, Active, Idle, Connect, OpenSent, OpenConfirm) exposed under `locale-services/{ls-id}/bgp/neighbors/{n-id}/status`.
(2) Match `lb_pool_latest` / `vs_latest` with **Networking → Load Balancing**, verifying ACTIVE / PASSIVE monitors (HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, ICMP) align with UP/DOWN/DISABLED/GRACEFUL_DISABLED/UNUSED pool states.
(3) During incidents, optionally run `get dataplane stats`—Splunk `pps_peak`/`drop_peak` must track CLI `DPDK` counters within skew budgets cited across bare-metal (**80 Gbps**/node ceilings) versus VM Large sizing docs.
(4) Compare `vpn_sessions_latest` with VPN dashboards enumerating `IKEv2` phase-1 / phase-2 `SA` lifetimes plus `dpd-timeout`/`lifetime` knobs reflected via Policy IPSec attachments.
(5) Review **Monitoring → Alerts** histories whenever `edge_ha_cluster_event` aligns with rehearsed **Maintenance Mode**/`CRQ`/preemptive drills documented inside `configure-an-edge-cluster.html` references—not every burst equals outage.

**Step 4 — Operationalise**
(1) Route `tier0_bgp_peer_not_established` to WAN/**Tier-0** SMEs; route `native_lb_pool_member_bad` to **LB**/**platform** owners referencing `lbs_ids` facets.
(2) Tie suppression lists to `tier0_latest`/`edge_nodes`/`CRQ` IDs covering graceful BGP drills plus **Edge** evacuations so north-south throughput dips track active-standby takeover—not mysterious blackholes.
(3) Archive PNG/CSV bundles tying `edge_risk`/`gbps_peak`/`pps_peak`/`vpn_sessions_latest` when Sev1 bridges close for **ECMP** investigations referencing **Tier-0** `locale_services` edits.
(4) Plot `dataplane_gbps`/`pps_peak`/`edge_cpu_pct` heatmaps contrasting bare-metal throughput envelopes versus VM Large (**8 vCPU / 32 GB RAM**) ceilings—distinct from **Distributed Firewall**/`vsip` siblings handled inside UC-18.2.1 scopes intentionally.
(5) Tune `Splunk Connect for Syslog` queues/acks during syslog floods—ingestion lag must never impersonate BGP/LB faults inside **`edge_risk`** tiles.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

• **Stale BGP JSON keys** — Expand `coalesce` chains after `fieldsummary` exposes camelCase gaps lacking `FIELDALIAS` coverage inside **vmware:nsxt:edge_bgp**—Active/Idle churn often traces stale `neighbor_id` post Policy refactor.

• **LB DOWN vs GUI GREEN** — ACTIVE monitor targets may lag `pool` subnet migrations—Splunk mirrors DISABLED/GRACEFUL_DISABLED administrative states mid-change windows.

• **Throughput dips during HA** — **Maintenance Mode**/`CRQ`/active-standby takeover shifts **Tier-0** **SR** ownership—suppress dataplane throughput alerts synchronized with documented **Edge** switches.

• **Drops minus optics faults** — Resize toward Extra-Large/bare-metal Edge when Medium SKU saturates north-south bursts before rewriting parsers.

• **VPN zeros with tunnels UP** — Inspect Heavy Forwarder `splunkd.log`/`python` HTTPS failures (429/502) truncating **vmware:nsxt:edge_vpn** payloads—not IKE alone.

• **HA syslog storms** — Planned preemptive drills mimic outages—cross-check rehearsal calendars before escalating split-brain hypotheses.

• **runtime_latest UNKNOWN near snapshots** — Edge VM snapshots stall dataplane probes briefly—stretch suppression across **vSphere** snapshot CMDB bridges.

• **Blank locale_services_seen** — Truncated Policy pulls often mean `NETWORK_ADMIN`/`infra` RBAC regressions post credential rotations—retest `tier-0s` GET privileges.

• **PRI drift on relays** — Extend `FIELDALIAS` overlays whenever relays prepend tokens breaking `BGP[NEIGHBOR]:`/`lb[VIRTUAL_SERVER]:` signatures VMware documents.

• **BGP cardinality explosions** — **ECMP** **Tier-0** duplicates neighbor paths across **Edge** peers—facet dashboards via `edge_cluster_id`/`site_code` lookups before paging SMEs nightly.

• **VPN jitter during WAN MTU sweeps** — Coordinate IKE `dpd-timeout` suppression windows with WAN engineers adjusting `PMTUD`/`MSS` clamps referenced inside IPSec chapters.

• **Synchronized pool DOWN rows** — ACTIVE monitors referencing unreachable `management` subnets mid-firewall refactor flash DOWN everywhere—confirm `monitor` subnets align before blaming backends.

• **Firewall sibling bleed** — Panels tied to this SPL must omit **vmware:nsxt:firewall**/`vsip` feeds—east-west `RULE_TAG` narratives belong inside UC-18.2.1 scopes rather than **Tier-0** north-south overlays here.

• **Drops with healthy optics** — Escalate NIC driver regressions via `support-bundle` evidence alongside Splunk captures before hardware vendor bridges fire blindly.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the outward routers and Edge appliances carrying campus WAN traffic and VIP flows into your fabric. When upstream BGP sessions wander away from steady handshakes, IPSec tunnels flutter, balancer backends blink unhealthy, or packet movers saturate, we surface it plainly so backbone engineers—not application squads—know where to start.
